title: Lastrea lanciloba
intro: Lastrea lanciloba, also known as the lanced-lobed fern, is a species of fern in the family Dryopteridaceae. The plant is valued for its potential health benefits, which include anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, and is traditionally used to treat conditions such as fever, cough, and respiratory issues. The bioactive constituents of Lastrea lanciloba include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and terpenoids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. In traditional herbal medicine, Lastrea lanciloba is commonly prepared as a tea or infusion, and is also used in the production of tinctures and extracts.
